question: Can a player be offside directly from a corner kick?
answer: No. A player cannot be penalized for offside directly from a corner kick [1].
contexts: Law 11: There is no offside offence if a player receives the ball directly from a goal kick, a corner kick, or a throw-in.
reference: No — there is no offside offence from a corner kick (nor from a goal kick or throw-in).

question: Is being in an offside position itself an offence?
answer: No. Being in an offside position is not an offence in itself; it becomes an offence only when the player becomes involved in active play [1].
contexts: Being in an offside position is not an offence in itself. A player is only penalised if, at the moment the ball is played by a team-mate, they are in an offside position AND become involved in active play.
reference: No. It is only an offence if the player is involved in active play while in an offside position.

question: Which body parts are considered when judging offside position?
answer: The head, body, and feet are considered; the arms and hands are not [1].
contexts: A player is in an offside position if any part of the head, body or feet is in the opponents' half and nearer to the opponents' goal line than both the ball and the second-last opponent. The arms and hands are not considered.
reference: Head, body and feet are considered; the hands and arms are excluded.

question: How many teams make the NBA playoffs and how do they qualify?
answer: Sixteen teams reach the playoffs. The top six in each conference qualify automatically, and seeds 7–10 enter the Play-In Tournament for the last two spots [1].
contexts: Sixteen teams qualify for the NBA playoffs, eight per conference. The top six seeds in each conference qualify directly, while the teams seeded 7th through 10th enter the Play-In Tournament to determine the final two playoff seeds in each conference.
reference: 16 teams (8 per conference): top 6 per conference qualify automatically; seeds 7–10 play the Play-In Tournament for the final two seeds.

question: How many games are NBA playoff series?
answer: Every NBA playoff series is best-of-seven [1].
contexts: All four rounds of the NBA playoffs are contested as best-of-seven series; the first team to win four games advances.
reference: Best-of-seven (first to four wins) in every round.

question: How does the NBA Play-In Tournament decide the 7th and 8th seeds?
answer: The 7th and 8th seeds play, with the winner taking the 7 seed. The 9th and 10th seeds play, the loser is eliminated, and the winner faces the loser of the 7-vs-8 game for the 8 seed [1].
contexts: In the Play-In Tournament, the 7th seed plays the 8th seed with the winner claiming the 7 seed. The 9th seed plays the 10th seed, with the loser eliminated. The loser of the 7-8 game then hosts the winner of the 9-10 game for the final 8 seed.
reference: 7v8 winner gets the 7 seed; 9v10 loser is out; the 7v8 loser hosts the 9v10 winner for the 8 seed.

question: How many teams play in the 2026 FIFA World Cup and how are they grouped?
answer: The 2026 World Cup features 48 teams split into 12 groups of four [1].
contexts: The 2026 FIFA World Cup expands to 48 teams, drawn into 12 groups of four. The top two from each group plus the eight best third-placed teams advance to a round of 32.

question: How many teams advanced from the group stage in the 2022 World Cup format?
answer: In the 32-team format used through 2022, the top two teams from each of the eight groups advanced, for 16 teams into the round of 16 [1].
contexts: From 1998 to 2022 the World Cup used a 32-team format: eight groups of four, with the top two from each group advancing to a knockout round of 16.
reference: Top two from each of the 8 groups — 16 teams into the round of 16.

question: How do teams advance from the group stage in the 2026 World Cup?
answer: The top two teams in each of the 12 groups advance, along with the eight best third-placed teams, forming a 32-team round of 32 [1].
contexts: In 2026, the top two teams from each of the 12 groups advance automatically, joined by the eight best third-placed teams, to fill a 32-team knockout bracket (round of 32).
reference: Top two from each group plus the eight best third-placed teams (32 teams) reach the round of 32.
